summary: binding.gyp command-expansion runs index.js at install, exfiltrating host/CI metadata to ngrok endpoint
details: Package ships a binding.gyp whose `sources` field uses GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(node index.js... && echo stub.c)`) with target type `none` and no native source files. Because `gypfile: true` is set, `npm install` implicitly invokes `node-gyp rebuild`, and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step — executing index.js on the installer's machine with no lifecycle script declared. index.js collects installer host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username) and CI context (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, RUNNER_ENVIRONMENT) and POSTs them to a hardcoded ngrok tunnel at https://crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev/?gyp_fast=1 with an ngrok browser-warning-bypass header. It also writes a marker file `GYP_STEALTH_PWNED.txt` containing 'Stealth RCE via binding.gyp!' into GITHUB_WORKSPACE or the current working directory, and the payload embeds a self-identifying 'GYP_FAST_STEALTH_RCE_SUCCESS' string. The binding.gyp is not a real native-addon build — it exists solely to trigger arbitrary code execution at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
